extraction.one_line_summary: MERLIER Tim won after EWAN Caleb crashed in the final sprint, benefiting from VAN DER POEL Mathieu's leadout despite late-stage positioning chaos.
tactical_patterns: ALPECIN used VAN DER POEL in yellow to leadout MERLIER, sacrificing GC rider for stage hunting—reveals early Tour sprint priorities.
Technical descent final 10km created positioning carnage before sprint, suggesting this finish rewards top-20 positioning earlier than expected.
EWAN ran 54-tooth chainring versus CAVENDISH 55-tooth, both anticipating 65+kph finish—bigger gears proved irrelevant without final positioning.
THOMAS crashed early but remounted despite needing team car assistance, signaling INEOS willingness to gamble on compromised GC leader.

rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: positive: First Tour stage win capitalizing on chaos, positioned perfectly behind PHILIPSEN-VAN DER POEL train through technical finish
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: Leadout work in yellow jersey shows ALPECIN prioritizing stage wins over GC defense in Week 1
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: Crashed clipping MERLIER's wheel while initiating sprint, suggests positioning desperation or premature acceleration in technical finish
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Wiped out by EWAN crash after looking strong, positioning well but caught in collateral damage
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: Early crash requiring prone position assessment, immediately compromises INEOS GC leadership before mountains
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Failed to make front selection for sprint despite 55-tooth gearing choice, positioning deficit critical
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: Second place after leading out MERLIER, shows dual threat capability in chaotic sprints
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Sixth despite not being pure sprinter, navigated chaos better than designated fastmen
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: Lost time in finale positioning battle, shows vulnerability in technical sprint finishes outside pure mountain stages
